message: I stopped by since I was nearby.
It feels terribly lonely and dreary, even though it shouldn't be any different from before.
The clear sky visible through the window only adds to the emptiness due to the darkness inside.
There's no one here except the Director; it seems there's no one to comfort the current Director.
Patricia looks considerably older than her age due to the shock of losing her.
The strong personality and powerful presence that seemed to overflow from her during the shoot have vanished. She looks just like an old woman.
It's so painful to see her so dejected that I'm at a loss for words.
I'm doing fine. Really, I'm okay.
I wonder what I should do.
Can you leave me alone? I have no energy to do anything right now.
You're terrible too, asking about my daughter.
I still haven't found her head.
For what purpose does the criminal...
What reason for my daughter's head?
It's a horrible story, too tragic.
I should have gone to look for her no matter what.
The last I talked to was
Ah, no matter how much I regret it, I can't take it back.
There's no point in thinking about it, but if only I'd been a better father,
it feels like my heart is being ripped apart.
Right now, I don't care about anything in this mundane world.
I don't care about such things.
kill them with my own hands!
Director, I truly understand that feeling.
What do you really know? You didn't lose a daughter, did you?
This pain is something nobody but me can understand. I—
I don’t want to think about anything.
If the victims had been Ginny or Cindy, it would have felt distant, but finding my beloved daughter in such a horrible state—there’s no way I could stay calm.
Talking about anything now is pointless; it's impossible to think straight until some time has passed.
I can’t believe I'm having to see such a scene again.
Watching a person crushed by sadness is painful.
Why did Patricia have to be killed? I can’t understand the culprit's intent at all.
Could it be the act of someone who holds a grudge against the director? Regardless, there’s no reason for Patricia to have to die.
I didn't know Patricia that well, but I can’t believe she was hated enough to be killed.
When the filming was canceled, I was so concerned about the director, but I wonder why she isn't here today.
Why doesn't Jennifer comfort the director who is crushed by Patricia's misfortune?
I think there are other things to consider right now.
I heard that when the director tried to marry Meryl, Patricia stubbornly refused to accept it.
Meryl has now lost everything and is in despair. She could do anything.
Even though her relationship with the director has ended, it seems she has no intention of coming to comfort her former lover.
No, it doesn’t necessarily mean it was the same perpetrator. However, the method of dismembering the corpse is identical.
If Patricia's case is the work of the same murderer as the series of horrific killings,
then a motive of resentment towards the director becomes clear.
Even after Guy's death, the crimes did not stop. It seems there is indeed a true culprit.
